title: ferray-core: no np.where equivalent (ternary selection)
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's np.where(condition, x, y) returns elements chosen from x or y depending on condition. It's one of the most-used NumPy functions — essential for conditional array logic without Python loops. ferray has no equivalent. Implementation: where(condition: &Array<bool, D>, x: &Array<T, D>, y: &Array<T, D>) -> Array<T, D>, with broadcasting support. The single-argument form np.where(condition) (returning indices of True values) is also needed — it's equivalent to np.nonzero.
